identity.description: An advanced hybrid numerical-AI agent that parses astronomical 1D FITS spectra, fits a baseline continuum, detects spectral lines, and performs physical analysis (redshift estimation, radial velocity calculation, and element mapping).

inputs.description: The raw astronomical FITS spectrum file to process. Supports multi-segment HST COS/STIS tables, JWST binary tables, SDSS files, or 1D IRAF image arrays.
Configuration parameters for the continuum baseline fitting loop and masking.

polynomial_order.title: Polynomial Order
polynomial_order.description: Degree of the polynomial to use for fitting the baseline continuum.

mask_regions.title: Manual Mask Regions
mask_regions.description: Comma-separated wavelength ranges to manually mask out and exclude from continuum fitting (e.g., '1200-1230,6500-6600').

outputs.description: The parsed spectral arrays, continuum fit, normalized flux, detected lines, and physical analysis report.

ai_analysis.summary: The target spectrum shows a strong Lyman-alpha emission line, indicating a high-temperature ionized gas region.

wavelength.description: Wavelength grid points of the processed spectrum.

normalized_flux.description: Normalized flux values (flux divided by continuum).

units_wavelength.description: Wavelength measurement units extracted from the FITS headers.

units_flux.description: Flux density units extracted from the FITS headers.

detected_lines.description: Centroids and properties of numerically detected lines.

ai_analysis.description: Astrophysical classification and element mapping report from the AI.

tags.name: Astro-Spectra Analysis
tags.description: Processes raw 1D FITS files to perform automated wavelength calibration, baseline continuum polynomial fitting, line centroid extraction, and astrophysical profiling.
examples: Extract and normalize the spectrum of the white dwarf GD71 from a MAST FITS binary table.
Fit the continuum using a 5th-order polynomial and identify emission line peaks above 3.0-sigma significance.
Estimate the cosmological redshift and radial velocity of a quasar by matching observed spectral line centroids to rest-frame transitions.
